mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces positive returns during late-cycle phases, or industrial production declines fail to predict negative equity returns when monetary policy is actively easing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 2.0% confirms disinflationary pressures that eliminate near-term tightening risks, allowing equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 60% of low-inflation observations, or the signal reverses direction when inflation drops due to supply shocks rather than demand normalization.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate profit margins, creating a structural upward bias in equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day equity returns during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising real personal consumption expenditures indicates unsustainable consumer leverage and impending demand collapse, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals improving capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving immediate risk-on flows and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative during recovery phases when industrial production expands, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in post-crisis environments.
